gate_type: capability check (view_woocommerce_reports OR export_woocommerce_orders) + wp_nonce admin-only
reasoning: Two gates protect the vulnerable path, both requiring elevated privilege. First, ajax_gate() at class-wc-order-export-admin.php:514 enforces current_user_can('view_woocommerce_reports') OR current_user_can('export_woocommerce_orders') before any method dispatch — unauthenticated users and subscriber-level roles die immediately. Second, the wp_nonce ('woe_nonce') used by check_admin_referer at line 546 is generated via wp_create_nonce('woe_nonce') and rendered into the plugin admin page HTML (view/main.php:24) which itself is only accessible to users who already pass the capability gate. There is no route by which an unauthenticated attacker or a standard subscriber can obtain the nonce value. The minimum role required is shop_manager (which holds view_woocommerce_reports) or a user explicitly granted export_woocommerce_orders. The path traversal is real but requires a shop_manager or administrator credential to exercise — it is not default-exploitable from the outside. Severity is downgraded to LOW because exploitation requires an already-elevated, already-trusted role that normally has broader system access. The original adversarial verdict confirmed this in its AUTH CONTEXT section: 'admin or shop_manager in practice'.
